justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus an extra BR-5 for manager authorization). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day advance notice (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities formally surfaced as escalations (accrual month type, retroactive window anchor, deduction timing). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated given the ambiguities noted.

observations.text: All 7 rules correctly identified with accurate invariant classification — no misclassifications
Clear distinction between structural invariants (balance integrity, contract boundary) and configurable HR policies (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice period)
12 acceptance criteria with precise Given/When/Then format, covering accrual, negative balance, advance notice, medical exception, rejection, contract boundary, and carryover
Three well-structured escalations surfacing ambiguities with options and recommendations — covers the expected retroactive window and contract-end-date questions
Rich edge cases including fractional balances, pending requests during carryover, contract amendment conflicts, and medical leave still subject to balance invariant
Bonus BR-5 (manager authorization as invariant: true) is a reasonable addition that shows domain depth
Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than distinguishing 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management' as separate bounded contexts per the rubric expectation
Confidence level of 72 is appropriately calibrated — acknowledges spec gaps without being artificially low

trials.justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus BR-5 as a bonus rule). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day advance (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classification — no misclassifications
Clear distinction between structural invariants (balance integrity, contract boundary) and configurable policies (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice period)
12 acceptance criteria with strong coverage of accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, and boundary conditions
Rich ambiguity surfacing: accrual timing definition, balance reservation semantics, pending requests at start date, forfeiture interaction with pending requests
Escalations include concrete options and recommendations, not just questions
Edge cases are thorough — holiday interference with working days, exact boundary conditions, concurrent request scenarios
Invariant justifications are well-reasoned, explaining why each classification is correct
Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than distinguishing 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management' as separate bounded contexts per the rubric expectation
Confidence level of 72 is appropriately calibrated — not overconfident given the gaps, not underconfident given the clear core rules

trials.justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus an extra BR-5 for manager authorization). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day notice (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities formally surfaced via escalations (partial month accrual, balance debit timing, carry-over with pending requests, holiday calendar). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 55 is appropriately calibrated given the acknowledged specification gaps.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classification — no misclassifications
Excellent invariant justifications that clearly distinguish structural integrity constraints from configurable policy
12 acceptance criteria covering accrual, negative balance, advance notice, medical exception, rejection, contract boundary, and carryover — comprehensive
4 formal escalations surfacing genuine ambiguities with options and recommendations
Edge cases are thorough and operationally relevant, including the medical leave + insufficient balance interaction
Confidence level of 55 is well-calibrated — acknowledges clear rules but flags real specification gaps
Added BR-5 (manager authorization invariant) beyond the expected 7 rules, showing deeper domain thinking

Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than separating 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management' as distinct bounded contexts — though this is a reasonable modeling choice
Did not explicitly distinguish labor-law-adjacent rules from HR policy rules as a classification category, though the invariant justifications implicitly capture this distinction
